$schema_note: CLIENT-PATH model map TEMPLATE — resolved per box at install against the CLIENT's OWN configured providers/keys. Chain 1 (bio authoring) needs a long-output-capable tier (~7,000 words => 10k+ output tokens; set maxTokens accordingly, e.g. 65536 on ollama-cloud). Chain 2 (HTML formatting) is mechanical and may ride a cheaper tier of the SAME client chain. NEVER an Anthropic / claude-* id here; NEVER the operator's keys. verify.sh runs a no-Anthropic scan (AF-PB-ANTHROPIC) over the shipped skill + the resolved map. The source workflow pinned both chains to google/gemini-2.5-pro via OpenRouter (already non-Anthropic) — kept ONLY if the client configured that provider/key; otherwise the client's own capability-tested tier is used. Never substitute a client's expressed model choice.

S1_bio_author.notes: Must sustain a 6,000-7,000-word single output. Example client configs: their OpenRouter key with google/gemini-2.5-pro (if configured), or their ollama-cloud long-output tier. NEVER claude-*/anthropic/*.

S3_html_convert.role: mechanical HTML formatting (chain 2, system = prompt 02 verbatim)
S3_html_convert.notes: May ride a cheaper tier of the SAME client chain. NEVER claude-*/anthropic/*.

client_sovereignty: The client's expressed model choice always wins and is never floored, capped, or substituted. This template is filled with the client's own provider ids/keys at install; it is never shipped with operator credentials.
